text:
Once upon a time in a small, vibrant town, there was an owl named Oliver. Oliver loved nothing more than watching the sun dip below the trees each evening. One winter night he heard a melancholy song that lingered long into the silence. Curious, he flew towards the origin.

There, under a pine owl sanctuary, was a wise old rabbit. "That's a haunting song," the rabbit said. "It speaks of dreams unrealized. Have you forgotten yours?"

Oliver Perched thoughtfully. "Just dreams," he sighed. "But dreams full of snowflakes that flap like wings."

The rabbit smiled kindly. "You've flown those dreams far too high." He waved his paw.

That night, while the moon watched, Oliver's dream flutters took flight. They drifted over fields of lavender that sang softly and forests full of enchanted sprites.

The sprites welcomed him, whispering secrets of the woods, and the fields bloomed with colors unseen. Winter turned into
